quick_skim.title: GLIP: Graph and LLM Joint Pretraining for Graph-Level Tasks
quick_skim.abstract: Graphs are widely used to model relational systems, with applications in domains such as social networks, finance, and biomedicine. Graph neural networks (GNNs) have become a mainstream approach for learning graph representations. With the rise of large language models (LLMs), recent studies have attempted to combine GNNs with LLMs. However, most existing works concentrate on node-level and edge-level tasks, while graph-level tasks, which require capturing more complex structural and feature information, remain relatively underexplored. Moreover, graph pretraining is a widely adopted strategy to alleviate the challenge of label scarcity. Most existing approaches are designed solely for GNNs such as GraphCL, leaving LLMs uninvolved in the process. To address these limitations, we propose GLIP, a Graph-LLM JoInt Pretraining framework for graph-level tasks. GLIP first performs graph augmentation to construct positive and negative pairs and introduces a multi-token selection strategy to identify patches informative in both structure and features. It further leverages a diffusion-based projector to enrich them with contextual information, enabling GLIP to capture signals from both global and local perspectives. Finally, GLIP employs a joint objective that integrates the LLM's semantic judgments with a contrastive alignment loss, ensuring consistent supervision at both the semantic and structural levels. After pretraining, GLIP is fine-tuned with limited labeled data for downstream tasks, and extensive experiments show that it outperforms state-of-the-art methods on graph-level classification and reasoning tasks. Our source code is publicly available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/GLIP.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: graph and LLM joint pretraining for graph-level tasks; applicable to drug interaction prediction with knowledge graph
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 图与大型语言模型联合预训练用于图级任务，可应用于基于知识图谱的药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 图与大型语言模型联合预训练用于图级任务，可应用于基于知识图谱的药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 图与大型语言模型联合预训练用于图级任务，可应用于基于知识图谱的药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: GLIP jointly pretrains graph neural networks and large language models for graph-level tasks, enabling transferable graph representations that can benefit drug-drug interaction prediction with knowledge graphs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有的图预训练方法主要针对节点级和边级任务，忽略了图级任务，且未充分利用大型语言模型。该论文提出GLIP框架，联合预训练图神经网络和大型语言模型，以捕获更丰富的图级结构和特征信息。在生物医学等领域的图级任务上，GLIP展现出优越的性能。该方法可迁移至药物相互作用预测任务，尤其适合基于知识图谱的图级预测。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有的图预训练方法主要针对节点级和边级任务，忽略了图级任务，且未充分利用大型语言模型。该论文提出GLIP框架，联合预训练图神经网络和大型语言模型，以捕获更丰富的图级结构和特征信息。在生物医学等领域的图级任务上，GLIP展现出优越的性能。该方法可迁移至药物相互作用预测任务，尤其适合基于知识图谱的图级预测。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: drug interaction prediction with knowledge graph
